summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that concurrent POST requests to the policy-data subscription endpoint trigger a Go runtime fatal panic (concurrent map writes) that crashes the UDR process.
expected_security_impact: Sending concurrent POST requests to /nudr-dr/v2/policy-data/subs-to-notify should trigger a Go runtime panic due to concurrent map writes in PolicyDataSubscriptions, causing the UDR process to terminate.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the UDR, started the server, and sent 50 concurrent POST requests to the subscription endpoint. The UDR process terminated with a fatal panic (concurrent map writes) as expected.
verification_method: The verify script starts the UDR, records its PID, sends concurrent subscription creation requests, then checks whether the process is still alive. A dead process after concurrent requests confirms the panic-induced crash. The exploit also attempts a follow-up HTTP request to confirm the server is no longer responding.
evidence_produced_by_run: The UDR process PID was recorded before the exploit and confirmed dead after concurrent requests.
A follow-up HTTP request to the server returned connection refused, confirming the server crashed.
The concurrent requests were sent without authentication (confirming the auth bypass co-factor).

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
